[{"name":"AEROSPACE ENGINEERING, B.S.","templateAssets":[{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as neither concentration, (Aeronautics and Astronautics), has lower division courses, nor does it have any areas of emphasis or free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites 
","groupId":"06c37228-0dc6-4ca9-88ca-01997cdd6d5a","position":5,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"19a577a9-fa0a-44da-ba44-01997cdd6d5a","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

COURSES","groupId":"031a7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 

 This major has no concentrations. This major has no areas of emphasis. This major has 16 units of Agricultural electives. 4 units must be taken from a specific list - lower division choices are listed. The remaining 12 units can be selected from any CAFES prefix except MSL. Although not listed here, this major has 12 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.


","groupId":"97db7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"d24c94dd-5a32-4fd8-8691-01997ce3ba3a","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have any Support Courses, therefore this section is omitted. The Other Courses section is also omitted for this major as it has neither concentrations, areas of emphasis nor electives. Although not listed here, this major has 7-8 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

","groupId":"6bdc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"591f14ce-930a-48db-9c3e-01997ce80571","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"98c6646e-e69d-4fb8-84fa-01997ce80571","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"6dd6e566-49f6-4897-9aff-01997ce80572","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no concentrations. This major has 6 areas of emphasis; Agricultural Engineering Technology, Agribusiness, Animal Science, Crop and Soil Science, Forestry and Natural Resource, and Ornamental Horticulture. This major has 24 units of approved electives, grouped according to area of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 22 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"b17fb683-3f39-4c2f-b9a8-01997ceaa4d3","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"6e946f95-30ab-4dcf-b1ca-01997ceaa4d3","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


In addition to the lower division Support Courses listed, this major requires the taking of one 3 unit course in Animal or Plant Production (ASCI, DSCI, or PLSC - except internship or enterprise courses) as a Support Course. This major has no concentrations, nor areas of emphasis. This major has 12 units of approved electives. This major has no free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"23ad10b3-f362-4962-b9d9-01997ceba895","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"a299a2de-1786-4766-8deb-01997ceba895","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"47dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


In addition to the lower division Support Courses listed, this major requires the taking of one 3 unit course in Animal or Plant Production (ASCI, DSCI, or PLSC - except internship or enterprise courses) as a Support Course. A potential third section - Other Courses - lists any lower division courses that are either in Concentrations, Areas of Emphasis or an Elective for the major. This major has no concentrations, nor areas of emphasis. This major has 12 units of approved electives. This major has no free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

","groupId":"48dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"MAJOR COURSES","groupId":"be1a7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"85b657ab-150f-4132-88ef-01997cedfb28","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"f750feac-5312-431b-8952-01997cedfb28","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"df135ae3-fed2-4076-b1ea-01997cedfb28","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Environmental Studies and Sustainability, Human Ecology, and Global Studies and International Development. Lower division courses which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 24 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

","groupId":"21dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"MAJOR COURSES","groupId":"901a7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"OTHER COURSES (CONCENTRATION/EMPHASIS/ELECTIVES)","groupId":"22027745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"55d89b9e-0f59-4ef3-ba41-01997cf1f8e1","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"ad8a9e4d-af5b-47a9-8004-01997cf1f8e1","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"1ddb76ed-6f46-4104-b647-01997cf1f8e1","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

INFORMATION","groupId":"ecb99b72-a543-46e6-b403-01997cf36c1d","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 

The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as it has neither concentrations, nor does it have any areas of emphasis. This major has 18 units of Professional electives. These may include: any EDES, ARCE, ARCH, CM, CRP, LA or ART course. Additionally, any course included in any College of Architecture and Environmental Design minor, or the Art minor, may be taken. This major has no free electives, although if any Professional elective courses are also used to satisfy GE requirements, additional units of free electives may be required to meet the total units requirement. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  


Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"e3c578f0-d57b-4bd7-bc27-01997d03e572","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"56181d97-f7d9-432e-9fab-01997d03e572","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no Support Courses, therefore this section is omitted. This major has three concentrations; Graphic Design, Photography and Video, and Studio Art. Lower division courses which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. The Studio Art concentration allows for the taking of any art course(s) not already used to meet major or concentration requirements (12 units, at least 8 units must be upper division), or select TH or MU courses (lower division courses listed). This major has no areas of emphasis. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in.

This major offers the choice of either 12 units of Advanced Biochemistry electives or 18 units of Polymers and Coatings concentration. All these courses are upper division. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 13-21 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Anatomy and Physiology, Molecular and Cellular Biology, and Ecology, Evolution, Biodiversity and Conservation. If no concentration is declared, a General Curriculum path is available. Lower division courses (including approved, department and additional elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations or curriculum are listed.This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 5 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"ebdb7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"TRANSFER INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"ecdb7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"55090bcb-ed07-43bc-a866-01997d1bd206","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"7f69fc6c-45a4-476c-80f1-01997d1bd206","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"7833df78-9d7e-40dc-adfc-01997d1bd206","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"7fd5e8f8-895b-4cd8-af12-01997d1d7b90","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"d88fbc46-3196-40d3-be1a-01997d1d7b90","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as it has neither concentrations nor areas of emphasis. Whilst this major has 7-9 units of Approved electives, all are to be taken at the upper division level. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

INFORMATION","groupId":"ab0fe403-2970-4c26-83c7-01997d1ef249","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"6d291107-30f3-4fcc-8713-01997d1ef249","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"859c63d4-dccb-463e-8048-01997d1ef249","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has nine concentrations; Accounting, Financial Management, Management and Human Resources, Information Systems, Entrepreneurship, Marketing Management, Consumer Packaging, Quantitative Analysis, and Real Estate Finance. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) are listed - these are limited to the Consumer Packaging and Quantitative Analysis concentrations; all other concentrations are solely upper division. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 21 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"98be5551-1336-4b2a-b456-01997d20daf9","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"8207d470-f9b4-4245-9712-01997d20daf9","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major

In addition to the support courses listed, this major requires a Physics elective (3 units, 200-level and above). This major has one concentration; Polymers and Coatings. If this concentration is not declared, an alternative of 15 units of Advanced Chemistry electives is available. The majority of courses are upper division; the one lower division course is listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 9-13 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"9cfe55fb-067e-4c64-ab54-01997d22af29","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"f7d50f38-a377-44e2-9ee6-01997d22af29","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as it has neither concentrations, areas of emphasis nor approved electives. Although not listed here, this major has 26 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no concentrations. This major has no areas of emphasis. This major has 15 units of approved electives. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"84dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"TRANSFER INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"85dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"aca3134e-7598-48fc-a75e-01997d256e90","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page:

COURSES (CONCENTRATION/EMPHASIS/ELECTIVES)","groupId":"8b027745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":4,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations. This major has no areas of emphasis. This major has 2 - 4 units of Approved Engineering Science elective. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

","groupId":"79dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"5761ca7e-0881-44f7-bc8d-01997d27246c","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"d4fb28d5-d015-4422-87b6-01997d27246c","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"62c9a706-d6c6-44f8-99d8-01997d27246c","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations. This major has no areas of emphasis. This major has a choice of Focus Areas (24 units). Lower division choices are listed. Although not listed here, this major has 32 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

INFORMATION","groupId":"15a8de87-509f-4651-bc71-01997d292a38","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"715f3ea4-3a79-456d-b927-01997d292a38","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"f148b805-38d8-4d50-8797-01997d292a38","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

INFORMATION","groupId":"d92694f8-c7bc-4c6a-a59e-01997d2aa4dc","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"ff9c206f-9b0b-4523-92f4-01997d2aa4dc","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"0b24b423-714d-49d9-bc57-01997d2aa4dc","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites 
","groupId":"a0a4166f-02a5-4c6d-bd92-01997d2aa4dc","position":5,"area":"General"}]},{"name":"COMPUTER ENGINEERING, B.S.","templateAssets":[{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"TRANSFER INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"cddb7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as it has neither concentrations, areas of emphasis nor electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has five concentrations: Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning, Data Engineering, Game Development, Graphics, and Privacy & Security. A sixth option of General Curriculum is also available. Lower division courses which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"a47640ae-7083-459a-9f40-01997d2d593f","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"e9f3195c-9b0c-45da-a9d3-01997d2d593f","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as it has neither concentrations, areas of emphasis nor approved electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"407b9753-0f02-4f5f-99f4-01997d2f1113","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"6e616cb2-c4e1-448e-94b3-01997d2f1113","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has 30 units of approved electives. Lower division choices are listed. Additionally, any DSCI course not already used, or any courses used in the following minors can be used as approved electives: Agribusiness, Agricultural Communication, Crop Science, Environmental Soil Science, Equine Science, Food Science, or Poultry Management. Although not listed here, this major has 5 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"56dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"5c944642-99e3-466a-aced-01997d305d5b","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has nine concentrations; Quantitative Analysis, Real Estate Finance, Accounting, Information Systems, Management and Human Resources, Marketing Management, Entrepreneurship, Consumer Packaging Solutions, and Financial Management. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) are listed; these are focused in the Quantitative Analysis and Consumer Packaging Solutions concentrations, all others are solely upper division. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 4-12 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.
