INFORMATION","groupId":"1446bca3-b9e7-421a-a20b-01997d31fa59","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Power, Radio Frequency-Microwaves-Photonics, and Systems, plus a General Curriculum option. Lower division courses which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"ed824583-524b-4ce4-ba45-01997d32f85f","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"8af33d99-8648-4b56-82bf-01997d32f85f","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


The Other Courses section is omitted for this major as there are no concentrations; the two optional areas of emphasis, Fiction Writing and Poetry Writing, do not have lower division courses; and this major has no lower division electives. Although not listed here, this major has 28 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has two concentrations, Geology and Hydrology. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) in the Hydrology concentration are listed. All courses in the Geology concentration are upper division. This major has 20 units of approved electives. Lower division choices are listed. Although not listed here, this major has 0-13 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"e2db7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"6d70ff0b-c79a-40e2-8aec-01997d355f7e","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"2d393539-431b-4276-ab2a-01997d355f7e","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"bbc43d06-5dbb-4ee8-b980-01997d355f7e","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has two concentrations; Watershed Management and Hydrology, and Wildlife Biology. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has 29 units of approved electives. Although not listed here, this major has 0 -12 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has two concentrations; Advanced Food Science, and Culinology. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. An additional approved elective for the Advanced Food Science concentration is: one quarter of a foreign language. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 4 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Watershed Management and Hydrology, Wildlife Biology, and Wildland Fire and Fuels Management. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. Additionally, for the Wildland Fire and Fuels Management concentration, any CSU-transferable course either (i) recognized by the Cal Regional Fire Academy, or (ii) a Fire Technology course, or (iii) an Emergency Medical Technician course, can be used as an approved elective. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 0-12 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has a General Curriculum option (40 units) or an Individualized Course of Study. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"f1db7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"bf8230cb-be93-4809-842a-01997daa767d","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page:

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has four concentrations; Design Reproduction Technology, Graphic Communication Management, Graphics for Packaging, and User Experience/User Interface. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no support courses. This major has no other courses (concentrations, areas of emphasis, nor electives). Although not listed here, this major has 29 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"c2db7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"a2a1e235-20e2-44c7-9c9b-01997daccc92","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"978d374b-847d-4535-bacb-01997daccc92","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"18e33c70-dc02-4f9e-a15b-01997daccc92","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has 13 units of Technical electives. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"11dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"617a5b0e-6c83-457f-819e-01997dae357e","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"433436e5-c92e-48f7-87b3-01997dae357f","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"5ee13857-fdcb-4dd7-b784-01997dae357f","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"a5723eb3-d536-46b0-a75e-01997daf7a03","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"3ff2e1f6-a598-4cab-a90a-01997daf7a03","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

INFORMATION","groupId":"d8b3ef22-d00b-4006-9bea-01997db1b055","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"51f737fa-f1f5-4955-9665-01997db1b055","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"012c74b7-00fd-4d2f-a36d-01997db1b055","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has 8 units of Professional Electives. These may include any course in the College of Architecture and Environmental Design, any course from a College of Architecture and Environmental Design minor, or any course from the Art and Design Department. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

INFORMATION","groupId":"97cd1c97-d6f0-4dae-89ab-01997db30596","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"4884a78a-1825-46c6-aa80-01997db30596","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"eb76ab32-b135-475b-ae15-01997db30596","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites 
","groupId":"d881dcaf-596c-4049-a19c-01997db30596","position":5,"area":"General"}]},{"name":"LIBERAL ARTS AND ENGINEERING STUDIES, B.A.","templateAssets":[{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"TRANSFER INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"22dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no support courses. This major has four Engineering concentrations; Computer Graphics, Electrical Engineering (Power), Industrial/Manufacturing Engineering - System Design, and Usability Studies. This major has four Liberal Arts concentrations; Interactive Communication - Cinematic Focus, Interactive Communication - Theatrical Focus, Publishing Technology, and Technical Communication. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 0-2 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

INFORMATION","groupId":"93683586-961d-4209-bd4e-01997db6e790","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"87d82071-5072-4340-adb0-01997db6e791","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"92a025e8-ce1d-41d8-9520-01997db6e791","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Health Promotion, Exercise Science, and Sport Science. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 9 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no concentrations. This major has no areas of emphasis. This major has 13 units of Technical Electives. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"4bf40593-c7a4-4f08-8f9f-01997db9b7a3","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"a0fe1f3d-ed18-4714-946b-01997db9b7a3","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"b30262de-0775-4e7a-8b3d-01997dbb8083","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"6a8a9c74-25cf-47c3-a471-01997dbb8083","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has 8 units of approved electives/Technical Breadth electives. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

INFORMATION","groupId":"075b1bda-e435-4ba3-b9e7-01997dc5de6b","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"98003095-dab7-4532-afcd-01997dc5de6b","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"acd80dfe-f0b3-4668-a5b3-01997dc5de6b","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Pure Mathematics, Applied Mathematics, and Mathematics Teaching. If no concentration is declared, a General Curriculum path is available. Lower division courses (including approved electives) which may be in one or more of these concentrations or curriculum are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 7 - 19 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

For this major, transfer students should take ME 129 & 130 instead of ME 228. Any articulation with ME 129 & 130 should be mirrored with ME 228, only listed in the “By Department” or “By Prefix” views. Subsequently, after transferring into the program, transfer students should plan on taking ME 263 + 264 during their first quarter on campus, (rather than ME 128 and ME 163 or any articulated equivalents), as these courses are specifically designed for Mechanical Engineering transfer students. Course outlines can be found here: https://catalog.calpoly.edu/coursesaz/me/


 This major has five concentrations; General, Energy Resources, Manufacturing, Mechatronics, and Heating, Ventilating, Air-Conditioning and Refrigerating. Lower division courses (including electives, technical electives and emphasis areas) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


","groupId":"2cdc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"b5217397-b6b8-4c93-bed1-01997dc9e0cf","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"fc38f34b-3102-4695-bbff-01997dc9e0cf","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"e2975bdf-0746-4a79-8548-01997dc9e0cf","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

COURSES (CONCENTRATION/EMPHASIS/ELECTIVES)","groupId":"63027745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":4,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has 19 - 21 units of electives. Lower division choices are listed. Although not listed here, this major has 10 - 14 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"52dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"SUPPORT COURSES","groupId":"47467945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"MAJOR COURSES","groupId":"c51a7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"1f4192e6-a273-4546-a4bf-01997dcc2f59","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"72cf09c8-7481-42fd-b152-01997dcc2f5a","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"87ef6d09-84f5-454e-81dd-01997dcc2f5a","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have support courses, concentrations, areas of emphasis, or electives. This major has 6 units of Major Ensemble at 100-level, and 9 units of Applied Study. Lower division choices are listed. Although not listed here, this major has 21 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"5edc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"05a32921-2bfd-4c19-b0ef-01997dcf4b7e","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"da4185e7-b06b-4d8f-b73a-01997dcf4b7e","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"a768252a-8f1f-4c48-aadc-01997dcf4b7e","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in.

This major has two concentrations; Applied Nutrition, and Nutrition Science. Lower division courses (including approved electives) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. In the Nutrition Science concentration, an additional approved elective option is one quarter of a foreign language. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 0-1 unit of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Ethics and Society, Ethics of Science and Technology, and Philosophy and Religion. These concentrations have no lower division courses available for articulation. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 40 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has no other courses (concentrations, areas of emphasis, nor electives). Although not listed here, this major has 32 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"7bdc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"TRANSFER INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"7cdc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"7cfe4aed-0b5f-429f-8f74-01997dd37ea1","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"9215217e-df56-444c-b24f-01997dd37ea1","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"231264b0-9eeb-40aa-b817-01997dd37ea1","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites
