INFORMATION & RESOURCES","groupId":"9ddc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Fruit & Crop Science, Environmental Horticultural Science and Plant Protection Science. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 5 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"9edc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"788e034a-930a-4018-adde-01997dd60679","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"f572d764-7371-4931-8144-01997dd60679","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"c564b7b8-b386-4c17-ba2f-01997dd60679","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; American Politics, Global Politics, and Pre-law. An additional option of an Individualized Course of Study is also available. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 24 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"4b1140da-1f8a-4f6d-82f7-01997dd72f41","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"6776c164-5353-445c-9238-01997dd72f42","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Event Planning and Experience Management; Tourism, Hospitality and Destination Management; and Sports and Recreation Management. An additional option of an Individualized Course of Study is also available. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 10 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"0f8ad069-3e92-4a2d-8004-01997ddb5a34","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"513c0503-f356-4875-aaf0-01997ddb5a34","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"9fd1732a-d919-42ff-9038-01997ddd5c2a","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"6dab0cbf-ffc2-433c-8296-01997ddd5c2a","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has 16 units of technical electives. Lower division choices are listed. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University.

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major allows for 24-30 units of a declared academic minor, (department approval required, may not declare a Spanish minor). This major has no support courses. This major has no other courses (concentrations, areas of emphasis, nor electives). Although not listed here, this major has 20 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above.

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major does not have concentrations or areas of emphasis. This major has 24 units of Statistics electives. Lower division choices are listed. Although not listed here, this major has 8 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"63dc7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"faa8b15c-03c7-4085-98ac-01997de1f472","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"090c2efc-6076-4070-88f4-01997de1f472","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"a93b1161-dc7c-469f-a86d-01997de1f472","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites

COURSES (CONCENTRATION/EMPHASIS/ELECTIVES)","groupId":"6a017745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Thank you for your interest in Cal Poly San Luis Obispo!

This articulation agreement displays the lower-division courses required in major and support. Students are advised to complete as much lower-division course preparation as possible prior to transfer. Cal Poly has specific selection criteria for transfer students for admissions, and we encourage you to check the selection criteria for the major you are interested in. 

GENERAL INFORMATION

  • University Catalog for the full listing of degree requirements for this major
  • Admissions website for current transfer admission requirements
  • Cal Poly website for information on our campus


ACADEMIC INFORMATION

  • Curriculum sheets & flowcharts for Cal Poly majors | this will show you information about concentrations if applicable in your major
  • AP/IB exams and test credits earned & policies on college-level examination program exams  
  • Academic Advising  
  • How to use Assist.org 


This major has three concentrations; Enology, Viticulture, and Wine Business. Lower division courses (including approved elective options) which may be in one or more of these concentrations are listed. This major has no areas of emphasis. Although not listed here, this major has 6-8 units of free electives. Please refer to curriculum sheets and flowcharts link above. 

","groupId":"b6db7145-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"MAJOR COURSES","groupId":"181a7945-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADMISSIONS INFORMATION","groupId":"8fedf413-63fb-4541-ade6-01997de4e7a6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

To be a competitive transfer applicant, it’s important to start by reviewing Cal Poly’s Selection Criteria for Transfer Students for your major of interest. This page outlines general admissions requirements for all transfer applicants and the required and desired courses for admissions to Cal Poly San Luis Obispo.  Once you know which courses are expected, use ASSIST to see how your completed or planned courses match Cal Poly’s. You can search articulation agreements by major, department, or course prefix. Keep in mind that credit is granted based on the academic year in which you took the course. 


Note: Not all articulated courses listed in ASSIST are required to be a competitive applicant—but completing the required courses in the selection criteria and as many recommended courses as possible will strengthen your application. 

  

This is not a static document; new articulation may be added at any time. The information provided herein is subject to change without notice and does not constitute a contract or the terms and conditions of a contract between the student and the institution or the California State University. 

","groupId":"b3834b2c-0ec7-4ea8-87ba-01997de4e7a6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"SEMESTER CONVERSION","groupId":"afc3ca3e-6c64-41b3-9ac8-01997de4e7a6","position":4,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

Cal Poly is currently on the quarter system, and all Cal Poly course units reflect that format. Starting Fall 2026, the university will switch to the semester system. If you begin at Cal Poly in Fall 2025, Cal Poly will make every effort to ensure a seamless transfer of your community college work, so that you make progress-to-degree. ASSIST agreements, flowcharts, curriculum sheets, and planning tools haven’t been updated yet to show semester-based information. For latest updates, visit Cal Poly’s official semester conversion page: 


  • Semester conversion website 
  • Specific College semester conversion websites
